chapter_title.en: Chapter on Fasting Upon Sighting the Moon and Breaking the Fast Upon Sighting the Moon

text: 1916 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُرَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا تَطَوَّقَ اَلْهِلاَلُ فَهُوَ لِلَيْلَتَيْنِ وَ إِذَا رَأَيْتَ ظِلَّ رَأْسِكَ فِيهِ فَهُوَ لِثَلاَثِ لَيَالٍ»

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1916 - Muhammad ibn Murazim narrated from his father, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If the crescent appears surrounded by a halo, it is for two nights. If you can see the shadow of your head in it, it is for three nights."
